message:
fix(round): Use string-based rounding for Python-compatible behavior

Replace scale-round-unscale approach with string formatting for
round(x, ndigits) when ndigits > 0. This matches Python's dtoa.c
algorithm behavior and correctly handles cases like round(0.005, 2)
which should return 0.01 instead of 0.0.

The naive floating-point approach fails because 0.005 * 100 = 0.5
exactly in f64 representation, but the actual decimal representation
is 0.005000000000000000104... which should round up.
